csharp.note: Pre-existing LLM flake (Query_ToolCall_LlmRequestsToolAndHandlesError), same as tester v4 baseline

plang.note: Initial run showed 14/19 because the installed plang binary (16:09) was stale vs source (21:20); after rebuild, 19/19

uncovered_files: PLang/App/modules/condition/elseif.cs (0% line coverage by C# tests; covered end-to-end by PLang test)
PLang/App/modules/condition/else.cs (0% line coverage by C# tests; covered end-to-end by PLang test)
coverage.notes: C# coverage via coverlet only measures C# unit tests, not PLang .test.goal execution. elseif.cs and else.cs are covered via PLang TestConditionIfRecordsBranchIndex{True,Else}Branch but 0% in the C# coverage report.

findings.issue: Ends with `assert 1 equals 1`. Claims to guard the AfterAction coverage subscriber but observes nothing. If the subscriber silently recorded nothing, the test still passes.
findings.impact: Coverage subscriber regression would ship silently — test name lies about what it guards.
findings.suggestion: Either reach into Testing.Coverage to assert file.read/output.write were recorded, or downgrade the comment to admit this is a coverage-exercise probe only (not a guard).

findings.issue: Asserts `%isolation_probe% is null` at entry, but no other test in the suite writes %isolation_probe%. There is no source of pollution to leak in, so a broken-isolation regression would not be caught by this test.
findings.impact: The headline 'fresh App per test' claim is not verified by this test (it IS verified by C# RunActionTests.Run_FreshAppPerTest_IsolationBoundaryIsFileLevel).
findings.suggestion: Restructure as a test pair: PolluteShared.test.goal writes %shared_probe%=1; ProbeIsolation.test.goal asserts %shared_probe% is null at entry. Alphabetical ordering ensures Pollute runs first. OR delete the test since the C# version covers the case.

findings.issue: Three .test.goal files have IDENTICAL step bodies: `read file /system/test.goal, assert %goalText% contains 'test.report'`. They check the same string.
findings.impact: No incremental discrimination. Dropping test.report produces 3 failures for 1 root cause. Inflates the green count from 17→19 without guardrails.
findings.suggestion: Keep one as a structural guard. Make the other two actually test what their names imply: WritesJunitXml should write a junit.xml fixture and assert its content; IncludesCoverageTables should assert the coverage table format; RendersFailureWithVariables should run a failing fixture and assert the Variables map in output.

findings.issue: No test covers the case where condition.elseif branch matches (b=1, body runs, branchIndex=1). TrueBranch covers b=0, ElseBranch covers b=2 with elseif evaluating false at b=1. The elseif-matches path with the NEW condition.elseif action is untested.
findings.impact: A regression where elseif-matches sets wrong branchIndex, or skips the body, or returns wrong branchLabel would not be caught.
findings.suggestion: Add TestConditionElseIfMatchesRecordsBranchIndex1.test.goal: set %x%=7; if %x%>10 ... else if %x%>5 set %b%=2, else ...; assert %__data__.branchIndex% equals 1 and %b% equals 2.

findings.issue: Name implies the Variables snapshot is verified end-to-end, but the assertion is only `%hasFail% is true`. A regression that dropped the Variables snapshot but kept Fail status would pass.
findings.impact: The Batch 5 headline feature (AssertionError carries %foo%=42) is claimed to be guarded but isn't.
findings.suggestion: Reach into %results[0].Error.Variables.foo% and assert equals 42. OR rename to TestAssertFailureReportedAsFail to honestly describe the assertion level. Coder self-flagged this.

findings.issue: Name implies 'when called outside test mode, tag is a no-op' but the test runs INSIDE the runner — it exercises the accumulate branch, not the no-op branch.
findings.impact: Name falsely claims coverage. The no-op branch (currentTest==null) is unreachable from a .test.goal and would need a C# unit test.
findings.suggestion: Rename to TestTagAccumulatesWhenInsideTest (honest about what it tests). OR delete since TestTagAccumulatesUserTagsOnRun covers the accumulate branch more thoroughly. Coder self-flagged this.

findings.issue: Test's RegisterCoverageProbe mirrors the OLD production filter (`IsCondition && IsFirstConditionInStep`). Production was changed to `IsIfHead` in coder v3. Comment at line 18 still references the old shape.
findings.impact: A regression in IsIfHead logic would not be caught by this test. The test still has value for its original purpose (d05c138d Step-propagation fix), but its 'production filter' claim is stale.
findings.suggestion: Update the subscriber to use `action.IsIfHead` and the comment. OR add an assertion that `IsIfHead == (IsCondition && IsFirstConditionInStep)` for the two-condition-if step shape so both filter semantics remain validated.

findings.issue: New enum↔string normalization branches have no C# unit test. Covered end-to-end by three PLang tests (Run timeout/fail, Assert) which rely on `where Status equals 'Timeout|Fail'`.
findings.impact: Minor — the end-to-end path caught a stale-binary run in my review. But a direct unit test would give faster signal on regressions.
findings.suggestion: Add OperatorTests.Equal_EnumLeft_StringRight_NormalizesToEnumName and the mirror. Single method, four lines each.

findings.issue: NoForeach asserts contains test.discover + test.run + test.report. RunsAllDiscovered asserts contains test.discover + test.run + does-not-contain foreach. Only the foreach-absence differs.
findings.impact: Minor duplication — not wrong, just redundant.
findings.suggestion: Merge or make distinct. Consider: NoForeach asserts foreach is absent (negative test). RunsAllDiscovered exercises the full pipeline with fixtures (positive test).

findings.issue: test.run timeout=1 maps TestStatus.Timeout correctly but does not cancel the child's timer.sleep. TestRunEnforcesTimeout passes because Status mapping is correct, but wallclock is 5008ms instead of ~1000ms. cts.Token is passed to childApp.RunGoalAsync but downstream handlers read Context.CancellationToken which was set at child-App construction and not rebound.
findings.impact: A single test with a 60s sleep inside a timeout=1 wrapper burns 60s of CI wall-time, not 1s. Real latent bug. Not a test-quality finding — the test correctly asserts Status mapping. Coder self-flagged.
findings.suggestion: Route cts.Token into the child App's root context before dispatching the goal. Out of scope for test-quality fixes — belongs in the next coder iteration.

summary: Suite is green at 19/19 PLang + 2271/2272 C# (same pre-existing LLM flake as v4 baseline). 10 of 19 .test.goal files are strong guards that discriminate under the deletion test; 9 are weak or duplicative (findings F1-F9). F1-F4 are major: a tautology, a false-green isolation test, 3 identical Report tests, and a missing elseif-matches test for the new architecture. F5-F9 are minor nits. F10 is a latent runtime bug (coder self-flagged, out of tester scope). No shipping blockers, but the major findings are real test-quality issues worth a cleanup pass before security review. Coder v3's architectural work (condition.elseif/else as first-class actions, enum-string normalization, test.tag return) is functionally correct and covered end-to-end.
